content: @Gary Guo I added klint to my workflow for the prelude check. I did not see any warnings for the prelude issues you fixed in your recent submission. Do I need to turn it on?
It should be enabled by default

It never gets further than the kernel crate. It says 

  WARN klint::atomic_context Unable to determine property for FFI function <some functin>

a lot, and then:

error: cannot infer preemption count adjustment at this point
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/kernel/revocable.rs:108:5
    |
108 |     }
    |     ^
    |
note: preemption count adjustment is 1 after this
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/kernel/revocable.rs:108:5
    |
108 |     }
    |     ^
note: while preemption count adjustment is 0 after this
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/kernel/revocable.rs:108:5
    |
108 |     }
    |     ^
note: which is called from here
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/kernel/devres.rs:285:9
    |
285 |         self.data().try_access()
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

The build stops because of the error.
Oh, did you use the flake
I need to disable the default feature from the flake
Yea, I'm all nix based so it seemed like the right thing to do.
Could it not be flags to the driver rather than compile time specified?
Good point
Pushed a fix. Can you have another try?

That gave a different result - one larger than my scrollback buffer. Lots of messages like this:

error[E0425]: cannot find function, tuple struct or tuple variant `Some` in this scope
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/kernel/module_param.rs:166:18
    |
166 |             set: Some(set_param::<$ty>),
    |                  ^^^^ not found in this scope
...
176 | make_param_ops!(PARAM_OPS_U16, u16);
    | ----------------------------------- in this macro invocation
    |
    = note: this error originates in the macro `make_param_ops` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)

The end is like so:

error: aborting due to 1469 previous errors

Some errors have detailed explanations: E0405, E0412, E0425, E0432, E0514, E0531, E0603.
For more information about an error, try `rustc --explain E0405`.
make[3]: *** [/home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/rust/Makefile:337: rust/doctests_kernel_generated.rs] Error 1
make[2]: *** [/home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/Makefile:1336: prepare] Error 2
make[1]: *** [/home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next-build-x86_64-klint-0.71.1'
make: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
error: Recipe `l` failed with exit code 2

If you have doc tests enabled you need to replace rustdoc with a binary of matching version (otherwise it cannot understand the rmeta file). I've added some additional explaination to the klint README.
Ah, I was thinking about that. It would be great if you could expose the oxalica derivation you are using in your flake.
The latest commit to klint should give you a klint-rustdoc symlink which is version-matched. I've also added a passthru.rustc so you can get the rustc derivation if you want.

Now I have this:

          rustToolchain = if rustVersion == "klint" then
            pkgs.rust-bin.nightly.latest.complete
          else
            pkgs.rust-bin.stable."${rustVersion}".complete;

Gary Guo said:

The latest commit to klint should give you a klint-rustdoc symlink which is version-matched. I've also added a passthru.rustc so you can get the rustc derivation if you want.

I'll check the readme again.
That builds without errors :+1: Now how do I get warnings about importing stuff already in the prelude?
Wait a sec, it disabled CONFIG_RUST
What does rustavailable say?

Gary Guo said:

What does rustavailable say?

It says I made a typo in a path. Trying to figure out where.

Now it's going, thanks!

error: this item is available via prelude
  --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/linux-next/samples/rust/rust_misc_device.rs:98:1
   |
98 | use core::pin::Pin;
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |
   = help: import with `kernel::prelude::*` instead
   = note: `-D klint::not-using-prelude` implied by `-D warnings`
   = help: to override `-D warnings` add `#[allow(klint::not_using_prelude)]`

Seems like the lints are configurable via -D/-A?
Yes, it's configured the same way as rustc and clippy lints
Andreas Hindborg has marked this topic as resolved.
